宗我都比古
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宗我坐宗我都比古神社'''（そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 宗我坐宗我都比古神社]（奈良県神社庁）。/そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}曽我町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（大社）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社}}。 通称を「入鹿宮（いるかのみや）」とも。古代豪族の{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}に関係する神社として知られる。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の2柱{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 :* '''曾我都比古神'''（そがつひこのかみ、宗我都比古神） :* '''曾我都比売神'''（そがつひめのかみ、宗我都比売神） {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}での祭神の記載は2座。同帳では「宗我坐宗我都比古神社」、すなわち「宗我（= 曽我/曾我：地名）に鎮座する宗我都比古神を祀る社」と記載され、祭神1座は宗我都比古神とされるが、もう1座は明らかでない。 === 祭神について ===
'''{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|第8代}}{{jalink|孝元天皇|Q333572}}|02={{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205}}|03={{jalink|蘇我稲目|Q595897|稲目}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|彦太忍信命|Q11487911}}|02={{jalink|蘇我満智|Q11251684|満智}}|03={{jalink|蘇我馬子|Q1191233|馬子}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|屋主忍男武雄心命|Q11465637}}
{{Small|（古事記なし）}}|02={{jalink|蘇我韓子|Q11625210|韓子}}|03={{jalink|蘇我蝦夷|Q1292368|蝦夷}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}|02={{jalink|蘇我高麗|Q1092497|高麗}}|03={{jalink|蘇我入鹿|Q736621|入鹿}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
宗我都比古神社の祭祀は、社名・神名に「ソガツヒコ（宗我都比古）」と見えることから、古代豪族の'''{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}'''（そがうじ）に関係すると考えられている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。蘇我氏は著名な氏族であるが、その本拠地（{{jalink|本貫|Q846706}}）は明らかとなっておらず、宗我都比古神社の鎮座地である{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}}曾我（橿原市曽我町一帯）に比定する説のほか、大和国{{jalink|葛上郡|Q11620858}}（{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}一帯）に比定する説、{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}}（{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}東半・{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}一帯）に比定する説の3説が知られる{{Sfn|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}{{Sfn|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}。そのうち1説目（当地）は、地名「曾我」や宗我都比古神社の存在、および『紀氏家牒』で{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|蘇我石河宿禰}}について「家{{sub|二}}大和国高市県蘇我里{{sub|一}}」とあることから、特に有力視される地域になる{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}{{Sfn|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}{{Sfn|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}。 前述の通り、『延喜式』神名帳では祭神を2座とするが、これについて『五郡神社記』では{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}・{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|石川宿禰}}の2人にあて、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間（{{jalink|1688年|Q7671|1688}}-{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）頃の社記では{{jalink|彦太忍信命|Q11487911}}・石河宿禰の2人にあてている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。これらは、いずれも蘇我氏の遠祖とされる人物になる。それに対して、現在では祭神をソガツヒコ・ソガツヒメの2神とし、その木造神像2体を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}に祀る{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。神名帳では「地名 + つ + ひこ/ひめ」の2座とする神社の記載は多く、宗我都比古神社の場合にもそれらの例と同様に、在地神であるソガツヒコ・ソガツヒメの2神が祭神であったと考えるのが妥当とされる{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] === 創建 === 創建は不詳。『五郡神社記』では、{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}（在位：{{jalink|593年|Q33889|593}}-{{jalink|628年|Q30592}}）の時に、{{jalink|蘇我馬子|Q1191233}}が{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}と{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|石川宿禰}}を祀る神殿を蘇我村に創建したとする{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。一方で社伝では、{{jalink|持統天皇|Q232026}}（在位：{{jalink|690年|Q23472|690}}-{{jalink|697年|Q23489}}）が蘇我氏の滅亡をあわれみ、{{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}}の次男である徳永内供には{{jalink|紀氏|Q11605075}}を継がせるとともに、内供の子の永末には祖神を祀るための土地を与えて社務・耕作を行わせたことをもって創建とする{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 なお宗我都比古神社一帯は、{{jalink|弥生時代|Q207858}}から{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前期の遺構である中曾司遺跡が広がることが知られ、境内付近でも多くの遺物の出土が見られている[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html 中曽司遺跡]（橿原市ホームページ「かしはら探訪ナビ」）。「中曾司遺跡」『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年。。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）牒によれば、当時の「宗我神」には{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}として{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}から3戸が充てられていた{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。この記載に基づいて、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}2年（{{jalink|730年|Q23563}}）の『大倭国正税帳』に見える、大和国に神戸を有する神社39社のうちの1社を当社に比定する説もある{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）における「宗我都比古神」の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}の従五位上への昇叙記事、および{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}）における正五位下への昇叙記事が見える{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大和国{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}}に「宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座 並大 月次新嘗」として、2座が{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}に列するとともに、朝廷の{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}では幣帛に預かる旨が記載されている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 『大和志』によれば、{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には「入鹿宮」と称されていた{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}40年）9月に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社}}に列した{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から従五位上（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「宗我都比古神」。 * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}）{{jalink|6月16日|Q2653}}、従五位上から正五位下（『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「宗我都比古神」。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 中門 * 拝殿 * 大神輿収納殿 * 社務所 == 摂末社 == [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22901 宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] 別伝・{{jalink|持統天皇|Q232026}}年間（{{jalink|690年|Q23472}} - {{jalink|697年|Q23489}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 入鹿宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月6日|Q2928}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=宗我坐
宗我都比古
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宗我坐宗我都比古神社'''（そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 宗我坐宗我都比古神社]（奈良県神社庁）。/そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}曽我町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（大社）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 通称を「入鹿宮（いるかのみや）」とも。古代豪族の{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}に関係する神社として知られる。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の2柱{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 :* '''曾我都比古神'''（そがつひこのかみ、宗我都比古神） :* '''曾我都比売神'''（そがつひめのかみ、宗我都比売神） {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}での祭神の記載は2座。同帳では「宗我坐宗我都比古神社」、すなわち「宗我（= 曽我/曾我：地名）に鎮座する宗我都比古神を祀る社」と記載され、祭神1座は宗我都比古神とされるが、もう1座は明らかでない。 === 祭神について ===
'''{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|第8代}}{{jalink|孝元天皇|Q333572}}|02={{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205}}|03={{jalink|蘇我稲目|Q595897|稲目}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|彦太忍信命|Q11487911}}|02={{jalink|蘇我満智|Q11251684|満智}}|03={{jalink|蘇我馬子|Q1191233|馬子}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|屋主忍男武雄心命|Q11465637}}
{{Small|（古事記なし）}}|02={{jalink|蘇我韓子|Q11625210|韓子}}|03={{jalink|蘇我蝦夷|Q1292368|蝦夷}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}|02={{jalink|蘇我高麗|Q1092497|高麗}}|03={{jalink|蘇我入鹿|Q736621|入鹿}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
宗我都比古神社の祭祀は、社名・神名に「ソガツヒコ（宗我都比古）」と見えることから、古代豪族の'''{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}'''（そがうじ）に関係すると考えられている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。蘇我氏は著名な氏族であるが、その本拠地（{{jalink|本貫|Q846706}}）は明らかとなっておらず、宗我都比古神社の鎮座地である{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}}曾我（橿原市曽我町一帯）に比定する説のほか、大和国{{jalink|葛上郡|Q11620858}}（{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}一帯）に比定する説、{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}}（{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}東半・{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}一帯）に比定する説の3説が知られる{{Sfn|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}{{Sfn|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}。そのうち1説目（当地）は、地名「曾我」や宗我都比古神社の存在、および『紀氏家牒』で{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|蘇我石河宿禰}}について「家{{sub|二}}大和国高市県蘇我里{{sub|一}}」とあることから、特に有力視される地域になる{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}{{Sfn|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}{{Sfn|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}。 前述の通り、『延喜式』神名帳では祭神を2座とするが、これについて『五郡神社記』では{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}・{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|石川宿禰}}の2人にあて、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間（{{jalink|1688年|Q7671|1688}}-{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）頃の社記では{{jalink|彦太忍信命|Q11487911}}・石河宿禰の2人にあてている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。これらは、いずれも蘇我氏の遠祖とされる人物になる。それに対して、現在では祭神をソガツヒコ・ソガツヒメの2神とし、その木造神像2体を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}に祀る{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。神名帳では「地名 + つ + ひこ/ひめ」の2座とする神社の記載は多く、宗我都比古神社の場合にもそれらの例と同様に、在地神であるソガツヒコ・ソガツヒメの2神が祭神であったと考えるのが妥当とされる{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] === 創建 === 創建は不詳。『五郡神社記』では、{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}（在位：{{jalink|593年|Q33889|593}}-{{jalink|628年|Q30592}}）の時に、{{jalink|蘇我馬子|Q1191233}}が{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}と{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|石川宿禰}}を祀る神殿を蘇我村に創建したとする{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。一方で社伝では、{{jalink|持統天皇|Q232026}}（在位：{{jalink|690年|Q23472|690}}-{{jalink|697年|Q23489}}）が蘇我氏の滅亡をあわれみ、{{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}}の次男である徳永内供には{{jalink|紀氏|Q11605075}}を継がせるとともに、内供の子の永末には祖神を祀るための土地を与えて社務・耕作を行わせたことをもって創建とする{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 なお宗我都比古神社一帯は、{{jalink|弥生時代|Q207858}}から{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前期の遺構である中曾司遺跡が広がることが知られ、境内付近でも多くの遺物の出土が見られている[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html 中曽司遺跡]（橿原市ホームページ「かしはら探訪ナビ」）。「中曾司遺跡」『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年。。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）牒によれば、当時の「宗我神」には{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}として{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}から3戸が充てられていた{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。この記載に基づいて、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}2年（{{jalink|730年|Q23563}}）の『大倭国正税帳』に見える、大和国に神戸を有する神社39社のうちの1社を当社に比定する説もある{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）における「宗我都比古神」の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}の従五位上への昇叙記事、および{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}）における正五位下への昇叙記事が見える{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大和国{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}}に「宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座 並大 月次新嘗」として、2座が{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}に列するとともに、朝廷の{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}では幣帛に預かる旨が記載されている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 『大和志』によれば、{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には「入鹿宮」と称されていた{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}40年）9月に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から従五位上（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「宗我都比古神」。 * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}）{{jalink|6月16日|Q2653}}、従五位上から正五位下（『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「宗我都比古神」。 === 境内 === * 本殿 * 中門 * 拝殿 * 大神輿収納殿 * 社務所 === 摂末社 === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|境内社}}]] * 八大夫{{jalink|稲荷神社|Q514480}} * {{jalink|戎神社}} * {{jalink|八坂神社|Q692714}} === 祭事 === * 祈年祭（{{jalink|2月22日|Q2351}}） * 例祭（{{jalink|10月6日|Q2928}}） * 新嘗祭（{{jalink|11月26日|Q3019}}） === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}曽我町1196 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085}}（近鉄）{{jalink|近鉄大阪線|Q1052545|大阪線}} {{jalink|真菅駅|Q6785665}} （徒歩約3分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=宗我坐宗我都比古神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 30 奈良県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=日野昭|year=|chapter=蘇我氏|title={{jalink|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|Q11420603|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{jalink|吉川弘文館|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|蘇我氏（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=蘇我氏|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{jalink|吉川弘文館|Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=佐伯有清|editor-link=佐伯有清|author=|year=2015|chapter=蘇我|title=日本古代氏族事典 新装版|publisher={{jalink|雄山閣|Q11659206}}|isbn=978-4639023791|ref={{Harvid|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=平井良朋|year=1982|chapter=宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座|title=式内社調査報告 第3巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=木村芳一|year=2000|chapter=宗我坐宗我都比古神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 4 大和 <新装復刊版>|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=978-4560025031|ref={{Harvid|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=『歴史読本』編集部|author=瀧音能之|authorlink=瀧音能之|year=2014|chapter=宗我都比古神社と蘇我氏|title=神社の古代史（新人物文庫297）|publisher=中経出版|isbn=978-4046001368|ref={{Harvid|瀧音能之|2014}}}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}} * {{jalink|入鹿神社|Q11389090}}（橿原市小綱町） * {{jalink|蘇我比咩神社|Q11625202}}（千葉県千葉市） - 下総国千葉郡の式内社。 === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Sogatsuhiko-jinja}} * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 宗我坐宗我都比古神社] - 奈良県神社庁 * 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sogatsuhiko-jinja}} [[JA:宗我坐宗我都比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11451946}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 宗我坐宗我都比古神社 |画像 = [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}曽我町1196 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 曾我都比古神
曾我都比売神 |神体 = 木造神像2躯 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（大2座）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 一伝・{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}年間（{{jalink|593年|Q33889}} - {{jalink|628年|Q30592}}）
別伝・{{jalink|持統天皇|Q232026}}年間（{{jalink|690年|Q23472}} - {{jalink|697年|Q23489}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 入鹿宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月6日|Q2928}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=宗我坐
宗我都比古
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宗我坐宗我都比古神社'''（そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 宗我坐宗我都比古神社]（奈良県神社庁）。/そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}曽我町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（大社）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 通称を「入鹿宮（いるかのみや）」とも。古代豪族の{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}に関係する神社として知られる。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の2柱{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 :* '''曾我都比古神'''（そがつひこのかみ、宗我都比古神） :* '''曾我都比売神'''（そがつひめのかみ、宗我都比売神） {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}での祭神の記載は2座。同帳では「宗我坐宗我都比古神社」、すなわち「宗我（= 曽我/曾我：地名）に鎮座する宗我都比古神を祀る社」と記載され、祭神1座は宗我都比古神とされるが、もう1座は明らかでない。 === 祭神について ===
'''{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|第8代}}{{jalink|孝元天皇|Q333572}}|02={{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205}}|03={{jalink|蘇我稲目|Q595897|稲目}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|彦太忍信命|Q11487911}}|02={{jalink|蘇我満智|Q11251684|満智}}|03={{jalink|蘇我馬子|Q1191233|馬子}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|屋主忍男武雄心命|Q11465637}}
{{Small|（古事記なし）}}|02={{jalink|蘇我韓子|Q11625210|韓子}}|03={{jalink|蘇我蝦夷|Q1292368|蝦夷}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}|02={{jalink|蘇我高麗|Q1092497|高麗}}|03={{jalink|蘇我入鹿|Q736621|入鹿}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
宗我都比古神社の祭祀は、社名・神名に「ソガツヒコ（宗我都比古）」と見えることから、古代豪族の'''{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}'''（そがうじ）に関係すると考えられている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。蘇我氏は著名な氏族であるが、その本拠地（{{jalink|本貫|Q846706}}）は明らかとなっておらず、宗我都比古神社の鎮座地である{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}}曾我（橿原市曽我町一帯）に比定する説のほか、大和国{{jalink|葛上郡|Q11620858}}（{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}一帯）に比定する説、{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}}（{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}東半・{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}一帯）に比定する説の3説が知られる{{Sfn|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}{{Sfn|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}。そのうち1説目（当地）は、地名「曾我」や宗我都比古神社の存在、および『紀氏家牒』で{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|蘇我石河宿禰}}について「家{{sub|二}}大和国高市県蘇我里{{sub|一}}」とあることから、特に有力視される地域になる{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}{{Sfn|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}{{Sfn|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}。 前述の通り、『延喜式』神名帳では祭神を2座とするが、これについて『五郡神社記』では{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}・{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|石川宿禰}}の2人にあて、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間（{{jalink|1688年|Q7671|1688}}-{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）頃の社記では{{jalink|彦太忍信命|Q11487911}}・石河宿禰の2人にあてている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。これらは、いずれも蘇我氏の遠祖とされる人物になる。それに対して、現在では祭神をソガツヒコ・ソガツヒメの2神とし、その木造神像2体を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}に祀る{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。神名帳では「地名 + つ + ひこ/ひめ」の2座とする神社の記載は多く、宗我都比古神社の場合にもそれらの例と同様に、在地神であるソガツヒコ・ソガツヒメの2神が祭神であったと考えるのが妥当とされる{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] === 創建 === 創建は不詳。『五郡神社記』では、{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}（在位：{{jalink|593年|Q33889|593}}-{{jalink|628年|Q30592}}）の時に、{{jalink|蘇我馬子|Q1191233}}が{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}と{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|石川宿禰}}を祀る神殿を蘇我村に創建したとする{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。一方で社伝では、{{jalink|持統天皇|Q232026}}（在位：{{jalink|690年|Q23472|690}}-{{jalink|697年|Q23489}}）が蘇我氏の滅亡をあわれみ、{{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}}の次男である徳永内供には{{jalink|紀氏|Q11605075}}を継がせるとともに、内供の子の永末には祖神を祀るための土地を与えて社務・耕作を行わせたことをもって創建とする{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 なお宗我都比古神社一帯は、{{jalink|弥生時代|Q207858}}から{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前期の遺構である中曾司遺跡が広がることが知られ、境内付近でも多くの遺物の出土が見られている[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html 中曽司遺跡]（橿原市ホームページ「かしはら探訪ナビ」）。「中曾司遺跡」『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年。。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）牒によれば、当時の「宗我神」には{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}として{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}から3戸が充てられていた{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。この記載に基づいて、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}2年（{{jalink|730年|Q23563}}）の『大倭国正税帳』に見える、大和国に神戸を有する神社39社のうちの1社を当社に比定する説もある{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）における「宗我都比古神」の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}の従五位上への昇叙記事、および{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}）における正五位下への昇叙記事が見える{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大和国{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}}に「宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座 並大 月次新嘗」として、2座が{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}に列するとともに、朝廷の{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}では幣帛に預かる旨が記載されている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 『大和志』によれば、{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には「入鹿宮」と称されていた{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}40年）9月に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から従五位上（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「宗我都比古神」。 * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}）{{jalink|6月16日|Q2653}}、従五位上から正五位下（『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「宗我都比古神」。 === 境内 === * 本殿 * 中門 * 拝殿 * 大神輿収納殿 * 社務所 === 摂末社 === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|境内社}}]] * 八大夫{{jalink|稲荷神社|Q514480}} * {{jalink|戎神社}} * {{jalink|八坂神社|Q692714}} === 祭事 === * 祈年祭（{{jalink|2月22日|Q2351}}） * 例祭（{{jalink|10月6日|Q2928}}） * 新嘗祭（{{jalink|11月26日|Q3019}}） === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}曽我町1196 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085}}（近鉄）{{jalink|近鉄大阪線|Q1052545|大阪線}} {{jalink|真菅駅|Q6785665}} （徒歩約3分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=宗我坐宗我都比古神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 30 奈良県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=日野昭|year=|chapter=蘇我氏|title={{jalink|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|Q11420603|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{jalink|吉川弘文館|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|蘇我氏（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=蘇我氏|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher={{jalink|吉川弘文館|Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=佐伯有清|editor-link=佐伯有清|author=|year=2015|chapter=蘇我|title=日本古代氏族事典 新装版|publisher={{jalink|雄山閣|Q11659206}}|isbn=978-4639023791|ref={{Harvid|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=平井良朋|year=1982|chapter=宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座|title=式内社調査報告 第3巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=木村芳一|year=2000|chapter=宗我坐宗我都比古神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 4 大和 <新装復刊版>|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=978-4560025031|ref={{Harvid|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=『歴史読本』編集部|author=瀧音能之|authorlink=瀧音能之|year=2014|chapter=宗我都比古神社と蘇我氏|title=神社の古代史（新人物文庫297）|publisher=中経出版|isbn=978-4046001368|ref={{Harvid|瀧音能之|2014}}}} === 関連項目 === * {{Expand Japanese|宗我坐宗我都比古神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Soganimasu Sogatsuhiko Shrine | native_name = 宗我坐宗我都比古神社 | image = Sogatsuhiko-jinja, haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Soganimasu Sogatsuhiko Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.519334|135.771167}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Soganimasu Sogatsuhiko Shrine'''|宗我坐宗我都比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 一伝・{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}年間（{{jalink|593年|Q33889}} - {{jalink|628年|Q30592}}）
別伝・{{jalink|持統天皇|Q232026}}年間（{{jalink|690年|Q23472}} - {{jalink|697年|Q23489}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 入鹿宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月6日|Q2928}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=宗我坐
宗我都比古
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宗我坐宗我都比古神社'''（そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 宗我坐宗我都比古神社]（奈良県神社庁）。/そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}曽我町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（大社）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 通称を「入鹿宮（いるかのみや）」とも。古代豪族の{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}に関係する神社として知られる。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の2柱{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 :* '''曾我都比古神'''（そがつひこのかみ、宗我都比古神） :* '''曾我都比売神'''（そがつひめのかみ、宗我都比売神） {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}での祭神の記載は2座。同帳では「宗我坐宗我都比古神社」、すなわち「宗我（= 曽我/曾我：地名）に鎮座する宗我都比古神を祀る社」と記載され、祭神1座は宗我都比古神とされるが、もう1座は明らかでない。 === 祭神について ===
'''{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|第8代}}{{jalink|孝元天皇|Q333572}}|02={{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205}}|03={{jalink|蘇我稲目|Q595897|稲目}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|彦太忍信命|Q11487911}}|02={{jalink|蘇我満智|Q11251684|満智}}|03={{jalink|蘇我馬子|Q1191233|馬子}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|屋主忍男武雄心命|Q11465637}}
{{Small|（古事記なし）}}|02={{jalink|蘇我韓子|Q11625210|韓子}}|03={{jalink|蘇我蝦夷|Q1292368|蝦夷}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}|02={{jalink|蘇我高麗|Q1092497|高麗}}|03={{jalink|蘇我入鹿|Q736621|入鹿}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
宗我都比古神社の祭祀は、社名・神名に「ソガツヒコ（宗我都比古）」と見えることから、古代豪族の'''{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}'''（そがうじ）に関係すると考えられている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。蘇我氏は著名な氏族であるが、その本拠地（{{jalink|本貫|Q846706}}）は明らかとなっておらず、宗我都比古神社の鎮座地である{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}}曾我（橿原市曽我町一帯）に比定する説のほか、大和国{{jalink|葛上郡|Q11620858}}（{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}一帯）に比定する説、{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}}（{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|富田林市|Q490928}}東半・{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}一帯）に比定する説の3説が知られる{{Sfn|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}{{Sfn|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}。そのうち1説目（当地）は、地名「曾我」や宗我都比古神社の存在、および『紀氏家牒』で{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|蘇我石河宿禰}}について「家{{sub|二}}大和国高市県蘇我里{{sub|一}}」とあることから、特に有力視される地域になる{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}{{Sfn|蘇我氏（古代氏族）|2010}}{{Sfn|蘇我（古代氏族事典）|2015}}。 前述の通り、『延喜式』神名帳では祭神を2座とするが、これについて『五郡神社記』では{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}・{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|石川宿禰}}の2人にあて、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間（{{jalink|1688年|Q7671|1688}}-{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）頃の社記では{{jalink|彦太忍信命|Q11487911}}・石河宿禰の2人にあてている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。これらは、いずれも蘇我氏の遠祖とされる人物になる。それに対して、現在では祭神をソガツヒコ・ソガツヒメの2神とし、その木造神像2体を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}に祀る{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。神名帳では「地名 + つ + ひこ/ひめ」の2座とする神社の記載は多く、宗我都比古神社の場合にもそれらの例と同様に、在地神であるソガツヒコ・ソガツヒメの2神が祭神であったと考えるのが妥当とされる{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] === 創建 === 創建は不詳。『五郡神社記』では、{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}（在位：{{jalink|593年|Q33889|593}}-{{jalink|628年|Q30592}}）の時に、{{jalink|蘇我馬子|Q1191233}}が{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}と{{jalink|蘇我石川|Q11625205|石川宿禰}}を祀る神殿を蘇我村に創建したとする{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。一方で社伝では、{{jalink|持統天皇|Q232026}}（在位：{{jalink|690年|Q23472|690}}-{{jalink|697年|Q23489}}）が蘇我氏の滅亡をあわれみ、{{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}}の次男である徳永内供には{{jalink|紀氏|Q11605075}}を継がせるとともに、内供の子の永末には祖神を祀るための土地を与えて社務・耕作を行わせたことをもって創建とする{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 なお宗我都比古神社一帯は、{{jalink|弥生時代|Q207858}}から{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前期の遺構である中曾司遺跡が広がることが知られ、境内付近でも多くの遺物の出土が見られている[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html 中曽司遺跡]（橿原市ホームページ「かしはら探訪ナビ」）。「中曾司遺跡」『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年。。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）牒によれば、当時の「宗我神」には{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}として{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}から3戸が充てられていた{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。この記載に基づいて、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}2年（{{jalink|730年|Q23563}}）の『大倭国正税帳』に見える、大和国に神戸を有する神社39社のうちの1社を当社に比定する説もある{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）における「宗我都比古神」の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}の従五位上への昇叙記事、および{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}）における正五位下への昇叙記事が見える{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大和国{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}}に「宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座 並大 月次新嘗」として、2座が{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}に列するとともに、朝廷の{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}では幣帛に預かる旨が記載されている{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}。 『大和志』によれば、{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には「入鹿宮」と称されていた{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社（神々）|2000}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}40年）9月に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|宗我坐宗我都比古神社二座（式内社）|1982}}。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から従五位上（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「宗我都比古神」。 * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}）{{jalink|6月16日|Q2653}}、従五位上から正五位下（『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「宗我都比古神」。 === 境内 === * 本殿 * 中門 * 拝殿 * 大神輿収納殿 * 社務所 === 摂末社 === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, Sogatsuhime-no-Kami |Sacred object = Two wooden deity statues |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Major, 2 seats)
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = One tradition: reign of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} ({{jalink|593|Q33889}}–{{jalink|628|Q30592}})
Another tradition: reign of {{jalink|Empress Jitō|Q232026}} ({{jalink|690|Q23472}}–{{jalink|697|Q23489}}) |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = Iruka-no-miya |Pilgrimage info = |Annual festival = {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Soga-ni-masu
Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Soga-chō, {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major shrine), and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{jalink|Emperor Kōgen|Q333572}}|02={{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205}}|03={{jalink|Soga Iname|Q595897|Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911}}|02={{jalink|Soga Manchi|Q11251684|Manchi}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Umako|Q1191233|Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{jalink|Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|Kaneko}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}|02={{jalink|Soga Koma|Q1092497|Koma}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Iruka|Q736621|Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Takaichi District|Q1117901}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{jalink|Katsuge District|Q11620858}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}}); or that it was in {{jalink|Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399}} (the eastern half of {{jalink|Tondabayashi City|Q490928}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, and the {{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Soga Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}} and {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} era ({{jalink|1688|Q7671}}–{{jalink|1704|Q6824}}) assign them to {{jalink|Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} (r. {{jalink|593|Q33889}}–{{jalink|628|Q30592}}), {{jalink|Soga no Umako|Q1191233}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}} and {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{jalink|Empress Jitō|Q232026}} (r. {{jalink|690|Q23472}}–{{jalink|697|Q23489}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{jalink|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899}}, to succeed the {{jalink|Kii clan|Q11605075}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}} through the early {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the *{{jalink|Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} as {{jalink|shrine households|Q11589711}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879|Tenpyō}} 2 ({{jalink|730|Q23563}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Six National Histories|Q1151168}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{jalink|Ten’an|Q1075611|Ten’an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 6 ({{jalink|864|Q25041}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in September {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 40), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten’an|Q1075611|Ten’an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper (*{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” * {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 6 ({{jalink|864|Q25041}}) {{jalink|June 16|Q2653}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” === Shrine grounds [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}} Soga-chō 1196 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami
Sogatsuhime-no-Kami |Sacred object = Two wooden deity statues |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Major, 2 seats)
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = One tradition: reign of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} ({{jalink|593|Q33889}}–{{jalink|628|Q30592}})
Another tradition: reign of {{jalink|Empress Jitō|Q232026}} ({{jalink|690|Q23472}}–{{jalink|697|Q23489}}) |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = Iruka-no-miya |Pilgrimage info = |Annual festival = {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Soga-ni-masu
Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Soga-chō, {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major shrine), and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{jalink|Emperor Kōgen|Q333572}}|02={{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205}}|03={{jalink|Soga Iname|Q595897|Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911}}|02={{jalink|Soga Manchi|Q11251684|Manchi}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Umako|Q1191233|Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{jalink|Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|Kaneko}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}|02={{jalink|Soga Koma|Q1092497|Koma}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Iruka|Q736621|Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Takaichi District|Q1117901}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{jalink|Katsuge District|Q11620858}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}}); or that it was in {{jalink|Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399}} (the eastern half of {{jalink|Tondabayashi City|Q490928}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, and the {{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Soga Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}} and {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} era ({{jalink|1688|Q7671}}–{{jalink|1704|Q6824}}) assign them to {{jalink|Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} (r. {{jalink|593|Q33889}}–{{jalink|628|Q30592}}), {{jalink|Soga no Umako|Q1191233}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}} and {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{jalink|Empress Jitō|Q232026}} (r. {{jalink|690|Q23472}}–{{jalink|697|Q23489}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{jalink|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899}}, to succeed the {{jalink|Kii clan|Q11605075}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}} through the early {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the *{{jalink|Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} as {{jalink|shrine households|Q11589711}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879|Tenpyō}} 2 ({{jalink|730|Q23563}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Six National Histories|Q1151168}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{jalink|Ten’an|Q1075611|Ten’an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 6 ({{jalink|864|Q25041}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in September {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 40), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten’an|Q1075611|Ten’an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper (*{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” * {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 6 ({{jalink|864|Q25041}}) {{jalink|June 16|Q2653}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” === Shrine grounds === * Main sanctuary * Middle gate * Worship hall * Portable shrine storage hall * Shrine office === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the grounds}}]] * Hachidayū {{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480}} * {{jalink|Ebisu Shrine}} * {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine|Q692714}} === Festivals === * [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}} Soga-chō 1196 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami
Sogatsuhime-no-Kami |Sacred object = Two wooden deity statues |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Major, 2 seats)
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = One tradition: reign of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} ({{jalink|593|Q33889}}–{{jalink|628|Q30592}})
Another tradition: reign of {{jalink|Empress Jitō|Q232026}} ({{jalink|690|Q23472}}–{{jalink|697|Q23489}}) |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = Iruka-no-miya |Pilgrimage info = |Annual festival = {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Soga-ni-masu
Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Soga-chō, {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major shrine), and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{jalink|Emperor Kōgen|Q333572}}|02={{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205}}|03={{jalink|Soga Iname|Q595897|Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911}}|02={{jalink|Soga Manchi|Q11251684|Manchi}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Umako|Q1191233|Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{jalink|Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|Kaneko}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}|02={{jalink|Soga Koma|Q1092497|Koma}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Iruka|Q736621|Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Takaichi District|Q1117901}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{jalink|Katsuge District|Q11620858}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}}); or that it was in {{jalink|Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399}} (the eastern half of {{jalink|Tondabayashi City|Q490928}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, and the {{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Soga Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}} and {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} era ({{jalink|1688|Q7671}}–{{jalink|1704|Q6824}}) assign them to {{jalink|Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} (r. {{jalink|593|Q33889}}–{{jalink|628|Q30592}}), {{jalink|Soga no Umako|Q1191233}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}} and {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{jalink|Empress Jitō|Q232026}} (r. {{jalink|690|Q23472}}–{{jalink|697|Q23489}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{jalink|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899}}, to succeed the {{jalink|Kii clan|Q11605075}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}} through the early {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the *{{jalink|Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} as {{jalink|shrine households|Q11589711}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879|Tenpyō}} 2 ({{jalink|730|Q23563}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Six National Histories|Q1151168}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{jalink|Ten’an|Q1075611|Ten’an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 6 ({{jalink|864|Q25041}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in September {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 40), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten’an|Q1075611|Ten’an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper (*{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” * {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 6 ({{jalink|864|Q25041}}) {{jalink|June 16|Q2653}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” === Shrine grounds === * Main sanctuary * Middle gate * Worship hall * Portable shrine storage hall * Shrine office === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the grounds}}]] * Hachidayū {{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q514480}} * {{jalink|Ebisu Shrine}} * {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine|Q692714}} === Festivals === * Kinensai ({{jalink|February 22|Q2351}}) * Annual festival ({{jalink|October 6|Q2928}}) * Niinamesai ({{jalink|November 26|Q3019}}) === Local information === '''Address''' * Soga-chō 1196, {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}} '''Access''' * Railway: {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} (Kintetsu) {{jalink|Osaka Line|Q1052545}} {{jalink|Masuga Another tradition: reign of {{jalink|Empress Jitō|Q232026}} ({{jalink|690|Q23472}}–{{jalink|697|Q23489}}) |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternate name = Iruka-no-miya |Pilgrimage info = |Annual festival = {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Soga-ni-masu
Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Soga-chō, {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major shrine), and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{jalink|Emperor Kōgen|Q333572}}|02={{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205}}|03={{jalink|Soga Iname|Q595897|Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911}}|02={{jalink|Soga Manchi|Q11251684|Manchi}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Umako|Q1191233|Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{jalink|Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|Kaneko}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}|02={{jalink|Soga Koma|Q1092497|Koma}}|03={{jalink|Soga no Iruka|Q736621|Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Takaichi District|Q1117901}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{jalink|Katsuge District|Q11620858}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}}); or that it was in {{jalink|Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399}} (the eastern half of {{jalink|Tondabayashi City|Q490928}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, and the {{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Soga Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}} and {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} era ({{jalink|1688|Q7671}}–{{jalink|1704|Q6824}}) assign them to {{jalink|Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{jalink|Empress Suiko|Q298057}} (r. {{jalink|593|Q33889}}–{{jalink|628|Q30592}}), {{jalink|Soga no Umako|Q1191233}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}} and {{jalink|Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{jalink|Empress Jitō|Q232026}} (r. {{jalink|690|Q23472}}–{{jalink|697|Q23489}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{jalink|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899}}, to succeed the {{jalink|Kii clan|Q11605075}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}} through the early {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the *{{jalink|Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} as {{jalink|shrine households|Q11589711}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879|Tenpyō}} 2 ({{jalink|730|Q23563}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{jalink|Six National Histories|Q1151168}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{jalink|Ten’an|Q1075611|Ten’an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 6 ({{jalink|864|Q25041}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{jalink|Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in September {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 40), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{jalink|Ten’an|Q1075611|Ten’an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper (*{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” * {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 6 ({{jalink|864|Q25041}}) {{jalink|June 16|Q2653}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” === Shrine grounds
