category|Category:Sogatsuhiko-jinja}} [[JA:宗我坐宗我都比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11451946}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine |Image = [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Soga-chō 1196 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami
Sogatsuhime-no-Kami |Sacred object = Two wooden deity statues |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major, 2 seats)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = One tradition: reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}})
Another tradition: reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Iruka-no-miya |Pilgrimage info = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Soga-ni-masu
Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Soga-chō, {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga Iname|Q595897|WD=Q595897|lt=Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Manchi|Q11251684|WD=Q11251684|lt=Manchi|1=Mitsutomo Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|lt=Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637|WD=Q11465637|1=Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|WD=Q11625210|lt=Kaneko|1=Hanko Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|WD=Q1292368|lt=Emishi|1=Soga no Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Koma|Q1092497|WD=Q1092497|lt=Koma|1=Soga no Koma}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Iruka|Q736621|WD=Q736621|lt=Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706|1=Korean clans}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{ill|lt=Katsuge District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}); or that it was in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|1=Ishikawa district}} (the eastern half of {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi City|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Soga Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}–{{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}) assign them to {{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (r. {{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}}), {{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|1=Soga no Umako}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} (r. {{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{ill|lt=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899|1=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro}}, to succeed the {{ill|lt=Kii clan|Q11605075|WD=Q11605075|1=Ki clan}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} through the early {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} as {{ill|lt=shrine households|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|1=Kanbe (social class)}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in September {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}) {{ill|lt=June 16|Q2653|WD=Q2653|1=June 16}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” === Shrine grounds === * Main sanctuary * Middle gate * Worship hall * Portable shrine storage hall * Shrine office === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the grounds}}]] * Hachidayū {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} === Festivals === * Kinensai ({{ill|lt=February 22|Q2351|WD=Q2351|1=February 22}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}}) * Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 26|Q3019|WD=Q3019|1=November 26}}) === Local information === '''Address''' * Soga-chō 1196, {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} (Kintetsu) {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}} {{ill|lt=Masuga Station|Q6785665|WD=Q6785665|1=Masuga Station}} (about a 3-minute walk) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon category|Category:Sogatsuhiko-jinja}} [[JA:宗我坐宗我都比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11451946}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine |Image = [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Soga-chō 1196 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami
Sogatsuhime-no-Kami |Sacred object = Two wooden deity statues |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major, 2 seats)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = One tradition: reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}})
Another tradition: reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Iruka-no-miya |Pilgrimage info = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Soga-ni-masu
Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Soga-chō, {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga Iname|Q595897|WD=Q595897|lt=Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Manchi|Q11251684|WD=Q11251684|lt=Manchi|1=Mitsutomo Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|lt=Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637|WD=Q11465637|1=Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|WD=Q11625210|lt=Kaneko|1=Hanko Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|WD=Q1292368|lt=Emishi|1=Soga no Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Koma|Q1092497|WD=Q1092497|lt=Koma|1=Soga no Koma}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Iruka|Q736621|WD=Q736621|lt=Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706|1=Korean clans}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{ill|lt=Katsuge District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}); or that it was in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|1=Ishikawa district}} (the eastern half of {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi City|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Soga Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}–{{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}) assign them to {{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (r. {{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}}), {{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|1=Soga no Umako}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} (r. {{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{ill|lt=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899|1=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro}}, to succeed the {{ill|lt=Kii clan|Q11605075|WD=Q11605075|1=Ki clan}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} through the early {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} as {{ill|lt=shrine households|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|1=Kanbe (social class)}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in September {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}) {{ill|lt=June 16|Q2653|WD=Q2653|1=June 16}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” === Shrine grounds === * Main sanctuary * Middle gate * Worship hall * Portable shrine storage hall * Shrine office === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the grounds}}]] * Hachidayū {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} === Festivals === * Kinensai ({{ill|lt=February 22|Q2351|WD=Q2351|1=February 22}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}}) * Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 26|Q3019|WD=Q3019|1=November 26}}) === Local information === '''Address''' * Soga-chō 1196, {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} (Kintetsu) {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}} {{ill|lt=Masuga Station|Q6785665|WD=Q6785665|1=Masuga Station}} (about a 3-minute walk) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Soga clan (Kokushi)}}}} ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Soga clan|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Soga-chō, {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga Iname|Q595897|WD=Q595897|lt=Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Manchi|Q11251684|WD=Q11251684|lt=Manchi|1=Mitsutomo Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|lt=Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637|WD=Q11465637|1=Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|WD=Q11625210|lt=Kaneko|1=Hanko Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|WD=Q1292368|lt=Emishi|1=Soga no Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Koma|Q1092497|WD=Q1092497|lt=Koma|1=Soga no Koma}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Iruka|Q736621|WD=Q736621|lt=Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706|1=Korean clans}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{ill|lt=Katsuge District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}); or that it was in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|1=Ishikawa district}} (the eastern half of {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi City|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Soga Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}–{{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}) assign them to {{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (r. {{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}}), {{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|1=Soga no Umako}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} (r. {{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{ill|lt=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899|1=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro}}, to succeed the {{ill|lt=Kii clan|Q11605075|WD=Q11605075|1=Ki clan}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} through the early {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} as {{ill|lt=shrine households|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|1=Kanbe (social class)}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in September {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January category|Category:Sogatsuhiko-jinja}} [[JA:宗我坐宗我都比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11451946}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine |Image = [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Soga-chō 1196 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami
Sogatsuhime-no-Kami |Sacred object = Two wooden deity statues |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major, 2 seats)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = One tradition: reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}})
Another tradition: reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Iruka-no-miya |Pilgrimage info = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Soga-ni-masu
Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Soga-chō, {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga Iname|Q595897|WD=Q595897|lt=Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Manchi|Q11251684|WD=Q11251684|lt=Manchi|1=Mitsutomo Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|lt=Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637|WD=Q11465637|1=Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|WD=Q11625210|lt=Kaneko|1=Hanko Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|WD=Q1292368|lt=Emishi|1=Soga no Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Koma|Q1092497|WD=Q1092497|lt=Koma|1=Soga no Koma}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Iruka|Q736621|WD=Q736621|lt=Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706|1=Korean clans}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{ill|lt=Katsuge District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}); or that it was in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|1=Ishikawa district}} (the eastern half of {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi City|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Soga Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}–{{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}) assign them to {{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (r. {{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}}), {{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|1=Soga no Umako}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} (r. {{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{ill|lt=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899|1=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro}}, to succeed the {{ill|lt=Kii clan|Q11605075|WD=Q11605075|1=Ki clan}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} through the early {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} as {{ill|lt=shrine households|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|1=Kanbe (social class)}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in September {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}) {{ill|lt=June 16|Q2653|WD=Q2653|1=June 16}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) – recorded as “Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami.” === Shrine grounds === * Main sanctuary * Middle gate * Worship hall * Portable shrine storage hall * Shrine office === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrines within the grounds}}]] * Hachidayū {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} === Festivals === * Kinensai ({{ill|lt=February 22|Q2351|WD=Q2351|1=February 22}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}}) * Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 26|Q3019|WD=Q3019|1=November 26}}) === Local information === '''Address''' * Soga-chō 1196, {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} (Kintetsu) {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}} {{ill|lt=Masuga Station|Q6785665|WD=Q6785665|1=Masuga Station}} (about a 3-minute {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市曽我町1196', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sogatsuhiko-jinja}} [[JA:宗我坐宗我都比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11451946}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine |Image = [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Soga-chō 1196 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami
Sogatsuhime-no-Kami |Sacred object = Two wooden deity statues |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major, 2 seats)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = One tradition: reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}})
Another tradition: reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Iruka-no-miya |Pilgrimage info = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Soga-ni-masu
Sogatsuhiko
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine''' (そがにますそがつひこじんじゃ[http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%B5%E8%A1%8C/%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E5%9D%90%E5%AE%97%E6%88%91%E9%83%BD%E6%AF%94%E5%8F%A4%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine] (Nara Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)./そがにいますそがつひこじんじや{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}/そがのそがつひこじんじゃ{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Soga-chō, {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also commonly known as “Iruka-no-miya.” The shrine is known as being associated with the ancient powerful clan, the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following two {{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}: :* '''Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami''' (そがつひこのかみ, also written 宗我都比古神) :* '''Sogatsuhime-no-Kami''' (そがつひめのかみ, also written 宗我都比売神) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed as having two seats of deities. The entry records the name as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine,” meaning “the shrine that enshrines Sogatsuhiko in Soga (= Soga/Soga/Soga: a place name).” One of the two seats is identified as Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami, but the identity of the other seat is unclear. === About the enshrined deities ===
'''Genealogy of the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||,|-|.||,|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{Supra|8th}}{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga Iname|Q595897|WD=Q595897|lt=Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Manchi|Q11251684|WD=Q11251684|lt=Manchi|1=Mitsutomo Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|lt=Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Yanu-oshi-o Takeo-gokoro no Mikoto|Q11465637|WD=Q11465637|1=Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}
{{Small|(Not in Kojiki)}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Kaneko|Q11625210|WD=Q11625210|lt=Kaneko|1=Hanko Soga}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Emishi|Q1292368|WD=Q1292368|lt=Emishi|1=Soga no Emishi}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!|!||!||!|!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|!|02|!|03| 01={{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}|02={{ill|lt=Soga Koma|Q1092497|WD=Q1092497|lt=Koma|1=Soga no Koma}}|03={{ill|lt=Soga no Iruka|Q736621|WD=Q736621|lt=Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||`|'||`|-|'}} {{familytree/end}}
Because the shrine name and deity name include “Sogatsuhiko,” the ritual practices of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine are thought to be connected to the ancient powerful {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. Although the Soga clan is a well-known lineage, its original homeland ({{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706|1=Korean clans}}) has not been definitively identified. Three main theories are known: that it was located in Soga (the area around present-day Soga-chō, Kashihara City) in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}} (the site of this shrine); that it was in {{ill|lt=Katsuge District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} of Yamato Province (around present-day {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}); or that it was in {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|1=Ishikawa district}} (the eastern half of {{ill|lt=Tondabayashi City|Q490928|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}} area){{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. Among these, the first theory (this area) is considered particularly persuasive, based on the place name “Soga,” the presence of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, and a passage in the *Kishi Kachō* that states of {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Soga Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}: “Residence: Soga village, Takaichi district, Yamato Province”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}{{Sfn|Soga clan (ancient clans)|2010}}{{Sfn|Soga (encyclopedia of ancient clans)|2015}}. As mentioned above, the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō lists two seats of deities. Regarding this, the *Gogun Jinjaki* assigns them to {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}, while shrine records from around the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}–{{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}) assign them to {{ill|lt=Hikofuto-oshi no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}} and Ishikawa no Sukune{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In all cases, these figures are regarded as distant ancestors of the Soga clan. In contrast, the present practice enshrines the two deities Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime, with their two wooden statues worshipped as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the Jinmyōchō, many shrines list two seats with the pattern “place name + tsu + hiko/hime,” and in the case of Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine as well, it is considered appropriate to view the enshrined deities as the two local tutelary gods Sogatsuhiko and Sogatsuhime{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === History === [[File:Sogatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. According to the *Gogun Jinjaki*, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} (r. {{ill|lt=593|Q33889|WD=Q33889|1=593}}–{{ill|lt=628|Q30592|WD=Q30592|1=628}}), {{ill|lt=Soga no Umako|Q1191233|WD=Q1191233|1=Soga no Umako}} built a shrine in Soga village to enshrine {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and {{ill|lt=Soga Ishikawa|Q11625205|WD=Q11625205|lt=Ishikawa no Sukune|1=Soga no Ishikawa no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. On the other hand, shrine tradition holds that {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}} (r. {{ill|lt=690|Q23472|WD=Q23472|1=690}}–{{ill|lt=697|Q23489|WD=Q23489|1=697}}), lamenting the downfall of the Soga clan, allowed Tokunaga Naigu, the second son of {{ill|lt=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899|1=Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro}}, to succeed the {{ill|lt=Kii clan|Q11605075|WD=Q11605075|1=Ki clan}}, and granted land to Naigu’s son Nagasue so that he might enshrine the ancestral deities and carry out shrine duties and cultivation; this event is regarded as the founding of the shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. It is also known that the area around Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine encompasses the Nakazoshi site, an archaeological site with remains from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} through the early {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}, and many artifacts have been unearthed in and around the shrine grounds[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/nakazoshi.html Nakazoshi Site] (Kashihara City website “Kashihara Exploration Guide”).“Nakazoshi Site,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture*, Heibonsha, 1981.. === Overview === According to a document from {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*, the “Soga deity” at that time was assigned three households from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} as {{ill|lt=shrine households|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|1=Kanbe (social class)}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Based on this record, there is also a theory that one of the 39 shrines in Yamato Province listed as having shrine households in the *Ōyamato no kuni shōzeichō* of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) corresponds to this shrine{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}}, there are records of Sogatsuhiko-no-Kami being promoted in rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, in {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), and to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}}){{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the *Engishiki* Jinmyōchō compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed in Takaichi District, Yamato Province, as “Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine, two seats, both major; receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals,” indicating that its two seats were ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} and that it received offerings from the court at the {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. According to the *Yamato-shi*, during the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was known as “Iruka-no-miya”{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine (Kami-gami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in September {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Soga-ni-masu Sogatsuhiko Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1982}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January
